question: Write a C++ program that dynamically allocates memory for an integer array of size N (where N is provided by the user). Populate this array with values from 1 to N, print its contents, and then deallocate the dynamically allocated memory.

test_cases.expected: Enter the size of the integer array (N): Populating array with values from 1 to 5...Array contents: 1 2 3 4 5
Enter the size of the integer array (N): Populating array with values from 1 to 1...Array contents: 1
Enter the size of the integer array (N): Array size N must be a positive integer. Exiting.
Enter the size of the integer array (N): Invalid input. Please enter a valid integer for N.
